zh.Great Leighs, Essex, England: 英國艾可格亞特 利斯
zh.Bailey Flat Farm, Buxton, Peak district,: 英國英格蘭德比夏爾布斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Mawde Le Bailly於1502年1月1日出生在英國越可爾奧文登時，她的父親John Mawde le Bailly25歲，母親Elizabeth Crowther22歲。她於1520年與Christopher Oldefeld在英國越可爾諾萊安結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1550年在她的家鄉享年48歲。

zh-hans.Great Leighs, Essex, England: 英国艾可格亞特 利斯
zh-hans.Bailey Flat Farm, Buxton, Peak district,: 英国英格兰德比夏爾布斯頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Mawde Le Bailly于1502年1月1日出生在英国越可爾奧文登时，她的父亲John Mawde le Bailly25岁，母亲Elizabeth Crowther22岁。她于1520年与Christopher Oldefeld在英国越可爾諾萊安结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1550年在她的家乡享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Bailly
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Emile, Marcel, Philippe.
French: occupational name for a steward or official, from Old French baillif. Compare Bailey 4.
French: habitational name from (Le) Bailly, the name of several places in the northern part of France.

variants.name: Bally
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Emile, Francois.  German Fritz, Kurt.
French and Swiss French: variant of   Bailly  . This surname is also found in Germany.
English: perhaps a variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Bailey
meanings: English: status name for a steward or official, from Middle English bailli ‘manager, administrator’ (Old French baillis, from Late Latin baiulivus, an adjectival derivative of baiulus ‘attendant, carrier, porter’).
English: habitational name from Bailey in Little Mitton, Lancashire, named with Old English beg ‘berry’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

English: occasionally a topographic name for someone who lived by the outer wall of a castle, from Middle English (Old French) bailli ‘outer courtyard of a castle’ (Old French bail(le) ‘enclosure’, a derivative of bailer ‘to enclose’). This term became a placename in its own right, denoting a district beside a fortification or wall, as in the case of the Old Bailey in London, which formed part of the early medieval outer wall of the city.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Mawde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Maud  , the usual spelling of the name in North America.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Mawde Le Bailly was born on 1 January 1502, in Ovenden, Yorkshire, England, her father, John Mawde le Bailly, was 25 and her mother, Elizabeth Crowther, was 22. She married Christopher Oldefeld in 1520, in Northowram, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1550, in her hometown, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Mawde Le Bailly est née le 1 janvier 1502, à Ovenden, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John Mawde le Bailly, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Crowther, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Christopher Oldefeld en 1520, à Northowram, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1550, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Mawde Le Bailly am 1. Januar 1502 in Ovenden, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Mawde le Bailly 25 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Crowther 22. Sie hat 1520 in Northowram, Yorkshire, England Christopher Oldefeld geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1550 im Alter von 48 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Mawde Le Bailly è nata il il 1 gennaio 1502 nel Ovenden, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, John Mawde le Bailly, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Crowther, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Christopher Oldefeld nel 1520, nel Northowram, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1550, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Mawde Le Baillyが1502年1月1日にイギリスヨークシュアイアオベンデンで生まれた時，父John Mawde le Baillyは25歳，母Elizabeth Crowtherは22歳であった。1520年にイギリスヨークシュアイアノーソウラムでChristopher Oldefeldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1550年に故郷で48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Mawde Le Bailly(이)가 1502년 1월 1일 영국 요르크실르 븐든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Mawde le Bailly(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Crowther(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1520년 영국 요르크실르 노르토램에서 Christopher Oldefeld(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1550년 그녀의 고향에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Mawde Le Bailly nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1502, em Ovenden, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Mawde le Bailly, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Crowther, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Christopher Oldefeld em 1520, em Northowram, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1550, em sua cidade natal, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Mawde Le Bailly родилась 1 января 1502 г. в местности овенден, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Mawde le Bailly было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Crowther, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Christopher Oldefeld в 1520 г. в местности нортоурам, еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1550 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 48 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Mawde Le Bailly nació el 1 de enero de 1502, en Ovenden, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Mawde le Bailly, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Crowther, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Christopher Oldefeld en 1520, en Northowram, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1550, en su pueblo, a la edad de 48 años.
